boundaries.rationale: These agent features all submit to, control, or inspect the same AgentTaskRunner task contract. Their repeated failure phrases therefore resolve through the same audited production boundary.
All listed provider choices enter the same route candidate, pre-output failover, and circuit-breaker implementation before provider-specific streaming begins.
These features are invoked or configured through the shared global-hotkey parser, conflict check, native hook backend, and macOS event-tap delivery boundary.

These speech features share the STT/TTS runtime health contract: provider synthesis, PortAudio input/output, optional model runtime verification, device resolution, and pinned local asset verification.
These external-agent features share the Codex/Claude harness session boundary and the guarded external transcript import, backup, append, and workspace validation layer.

These chat, history, project, model-route, and conversation-owner features run through the same guarded ChatWindow state machine, supervisor brain.chat stream, and atomic conversation-store persistence boundary.
These conversation-selected actions use the same ChatWindow index validation and no-op stale-selection guard.
These project-selected chat actions share the ChatWindow project-id validation boundary.
These message-selected chat actions share the ChatWindow retained-message index validation boundary.
These setup, profile, appearance, language, and Settings actions use the same settings-env reader, save transaction, cancel semantics, and live config reload boundary.
These generic Settings actions reject unknown keys and invalid fixed-choice values through the shared settings widget/runtime normalization layer.
These language selectors share the fixed-language combo and runtime language normalizer.
These appearance selectors share fixed theme/color selection widgets that do not activate unknown values.
These numeric appearance controls share safe numeric parsing/default behavior in the Settings runtime.
These add-on UI, hook, tool, settings, and MCP features share manifest loading, permission gating, dependency provisioning, the isolated add-on host, and MCP bridge protocol containment.
These non-MCP add-on features share the AddonManager enabled flag and supported add-on API version check.
These MCP grouping/discovery features share the MCP server enabled gate and JSON-RPC protocol compatibility boundary.

These retrieval, save, edit, viewer, and memory-tool features share the same enabled gate, JSON fact store, quality/deduplication filter, project scoping, retrieval router, and memory-model fallback chain.

These local-file modes, browsing actions, model tools, and approval actions share the resolved-root workspace, blocked-glob filter, in-band OS error containment, approval callback, and post-approval stale-file check.

These model selectors, route editors, connection search actions, provider adapters, streamers, cooldowns, and warning surfaces converge on the same route parser, configuration check, provider adapter, cooldown circuit breaker, and no-partial-output fallback loop.
These caller and conversation context controls share the supervisor's normalized context policy, native snapshot containment, stale-selection gate, preview state, and bounded query-context assembler.

These screenshot modes, snip actions, capture tools, and screenshot attachments share the native capture worker, strict region normalization, Qt virtual-desktop coordinate conversion, live screen DPR lookup, and supervisor capture containment.
These rewrite, picker, clipboard-context, and paste-back actions share the captured focus token, native clipboard write/restore path, target activation, and synthetic paste dispatch.
These GitHub metadata, issue, tool-context, and conversation-context functions share the GitHub OAuth token lookup, strict owner/repository identifier parser, and bounded GitHub REST adapter.

These web search, page retrieval, browser context, and source-mode functions share the caller allow-list, browser-source detector, optional fetch adapter, format normalization, and per-turn tool-output budget.

These send-mode, advanced-filter, and crash-report redaction functions share the built-in detector, optional local privacy runtime, post-scrub leak invariant, review decision gate, and custom-pattern parser.

These picker, caller, model-tool, and conversation document-context functions share the brain active-document handler, platform window/path discovery, optional accessibility/automation readers, and bounded document reader.
These persistent-context and per-conversation Selection controls share the native focus snapshot, platform accessibility selection reader, active-owner check, and empty/stale selection contract.

These model-returned, generated, and user-attached image functions share attachment base64 validation, image magic validation, size limits, file-reference loading, and the vision-route capability cache.
These intent and conversation image-context controls share attachment payload validation and the same vision-route capability/rejection path.

Setup and Settings ChatGPT sign-in invoke the same browser PKCE module and streaming brain handler; browser launch failure, expiry, cancellation, provider/network errors, and rejected scopes all finish through an in-band auth result.

Selected-text capture and the What is this, Explain simply, How do I fix this, Simplify, and Improve tone actions enter the same snapshot/context assembly, provider request, cancellation, guarded UI render, and optional rewrite paste-back lifecycle.

The shared error formatter now classifies empty input/context, route and network failures, cancellation, render failures, and paste failures into redacted user-facing messages with concrete recovery steps.

Filtering and expanding connection rows are local widget operations over already-loaded row metadata; an executable isolation test makes credential, network, provider, account-permission, and API adapters fail if touched and proves both operations remain functional without them.
Intent screenshot attachment and pending chat attachment context now share the same stat/open race, regular-file policy, suffix allow-list, read-permission, and byte-limit adapter.

Kokoro repair/update and post-install asset preparation use the same user-confirmed download path; provider, storage, permission, and dependency exceptions are returned as failed results, while cancellation stops before any worker starts.
Read Selection Aloud captures through the supervisor/native selection boundary and must stop before TTS while presenting a controlled notice for every accessibility/backend fault.
The MCP get_selected_text operation now converts focus, accessibility, permission, application, and platform backend failures into isError content so the long-lived protocol process keeps serving.

ChatGPT, GitHub, and dedicated Copilot sign-out actions now share a guarded clear-and-read-back contract, so keychain locks/unavailability/removal errors and credentials that remain after clearing cannot be reported as success.

The connection-level and individual-route model refresh buttons now share one safe live-list wrapper; unsupported providers, credentials, offline/rate-limited APIs, and response-shape changes return an error while the UI preserves curated models.

The Settings Test TTS action runs provider synthesis inside the shared TTS diagnostic wrapper, which contains authentication, endpoint, network, model, rate-limit, and unsupported-capability exceptions.

The Settings Image-model and Memory-model test actions both call the shared route configuration/probe wrapper, which converts authentication, endpoint, network, model, rate-limit, and capability exceptions into deterministic failed diagnostics.
The Copilot Test Connection action enters the same provider route fault classifier and pre-output fallback contract already used by live provider calls.
The ChatGPT and GitHub settings rows share the brain auth-status handler, including local token validation and exception containment around credential/provider status reads.

The intent-picker and chat-preview functions share deferred labels for unfetched sources, a local model-independent token heuristic, snapshot exception containment, and a fresh context capture on the real send path.

The context Git summary plus the model-facing git_status and git_diff tools share one read-only subprocess adapter, an explicit current-folder pathspec, working-folder validation, and the same output cap.
These add-on, log, crash-report, attachment, and general reveal actions share the resolved-path existence check and native Explorer/Finder/xdg-open launcher.
ElevenLabs, live voice, Kokoro, and faster-whisper are installed through the same staged optional-package runner, status writer, post-install verifier, and confirmation gate.
Conversation rename validates the proposed title before mutation and uses the atomic conversation persistence callback with an in-memory rollback when storage fails.
Conversation deletion verifies the selected record, active-stream lock, and confirmation before mutation, then restores the removed record if the atomic conversation persistence callback fails.

GitHub device OAuth exposes a copyable verification URL/code without requiring browser launch, contains initiation/polling errors, checks expiry and provider scope errors, and stops polling before token storage when cancelled.
The advanced privacy installer has one guarded preparation/download/verification entry point with durable status output, and Settings requires explicit confirmation before starting it.
Advanced privacy removal is confirmation-gated, treats an already-missing dedicated model directory as safe, and surfaces all filesystem cleanup exceptions without changing unrelated state.
Folder installation is selected through one guarded add-on manager action; cancellation stops before the distribution adapter and all adapter/provision faults are shown in-band.
Archive installation uses one guarded add-on distribution action that converts source, storage, permission, and dependency exceptions into a visible failure.
Dependency installation/rebuild is gated by explicit approval and the manager returns or raises all environment-provision failures through one guarded UI action.

Release and explicitly selected source-checkout removal use the same exact-target plan and detached renderer; the matrix runs both plan kinds through confirmation and launch faults, while renderer assertions prove missing-target, retry, partial-failure logging, and self-cleanup behavior.

The same launch function writes both platform helpers, launches them detached, removes an unlaunched helper after every setup/permission/process failure, and the rendered helper skips missing targets, records partial cleanup, and removes itself.

Setup navigation, TTS/STT choices, and icon-state guidance share the stateless onboarding page model; the test drives every page and persists the choices with both absent and corrupt prior profile state.
All four app workers start through WispSupervisor.start_all; every executable/dependency/timeout/stale-process/lock/permission/crash fault now rolls back the entire partial process set.

Packaged/source/autostart/setup/status/quit launch paths converge on supervisor main: the workflow proves duplicate-lock refusal, crash containment, and final shutdown, while the startup matrix proves partial-start rollback on permission/interruption faults.
Every listed lifecycle uses the same supervisor shutdown loop, which continues through a worker exception and force-audits the complete managed process snapshot.

These supervisor-backed launch modes share the same worker exit callback and crash-log lifecycle; the workflow injects a UI worker exit, records diagnostics, keeps shutdown deterministic, and returns in-band.

Clear-context is an in-memory user action rather than a filesystem deletion: it is safe when already empty, requires no secondary confirmation/elevation, clears local buffers before the guarded UI call, and remains cleared for every UI/storage-style exception.

Both inventory entries invoke SettingsDialog._reset_all; the matrix verifies cancellation, idempotent missing-file reset, and locked/elevation/storage/process/partial filesystem exceptions through the real confirmation and reset entry point.

Custom-profile deletion now computes one atomic env transaction and changes the dialog model only after persistence succeeds; the production entry-point matrix covers stale targets, cancellation, and every filesystem cleanup fault.

Fact deletion is confirmation-gated and now removes the UI row only from the successful background callback; the matrix injects every store/permission/process/partial-cleanup exception and proves the fact stays visible and retryable.

All Settings writes finish through SettingsDialog._apply_settings; its executable matrix injects each validation/store fault, verifies failure remains in-band, and proves the existing settings file is byte-for-byte unchanged.

Create and rename use the same profile env transaction. Their production-action matrices cover empty, normalized-invalid, duplicate, read-only, locked, corrupt, and interrupted writes while proving disk and the dialog model remain unchanged on failure.

Fact project changes use the memory row's guarded background update; the matrix rejects empty input and injects invalid/duplicate/read-only/locked/corrupt/interrupted store failures while keeping the editor alive and retryable.

Connection add/alias/key/clear/custom-endpoint/masking actions share the Settings connection rows and credential adapter. The matrix drives those production actions while injecting validation, keychain, endpoint, network, and account faults; the shared settings transaction separately proves persistence failure containment.

The onboarding Finish action now validates the profile/custom URL, guards keychain storage, and contains prior-env reads and settings writes. Its matrix injects all six setup-provider failure classes and proves the wizard stays open for correction or retry.

These icon, picker, bubble, drag-row, delay, and standard-window functions all execute on Wisp's Qt surface/event-loop contract. Production tests prove hidden-surface restoration, destroyed-widget replacement, input/compositor routing, stale/off-screen geometry correction, freeze detection, and contained window-manager refusal; each function's normal workflow separately drives its own action.

Last chat, Memory, Settings, provider controls, and Quit are built on the same tray/menu QAction dispatch surface. The matrix disables native tray support, rebuilds a destroyed tray/menu, verifies callbacks, and proves missing or unresponsive UI consumers never block QAction dispatch.

Streaming, reveal, speed, drag, snap, selection, and copy share SpeechBubble's reply state, timers, render pipeline, and external callbacks. The matrix injects every callback/timer/render fault and proves a later notice still renders; the stale-speech test proves older stream state cannot follow a newer reply.

Listening/transcript/progress/answer/warning/error status text uses the same guarded SpeechBubble timers, render validation, and callbacks; the matrix proves all faults remain contained and the real UI worker watchdog detects a blocked event loop.

Packaged checks, packaged download/apply, and source origin/main pulls share the Settings asynchronous updater carrier and status controls. The matrix runs all three production actions through network/source/disk/permission/verification/dependency faults and verifies explicit apply cancellation never starts the helper.

The repeated metadata/artifact failure set is executed as one multi-surface matrix, not inferred from wording: every fault invokes installed-version fallback, update error status, crash-bundle creation/review, runtime event logging, worker-status construction, and exact uninstall-plan confirmation.

Crash-bundle exclusion is deliberately independent of connection settings, keychain, endpoints, network, and account state. The production bundle test makes all three private/network adapters fatal if touched and still verifies chats/settings/keychain-like contents are absent from the ZIP.

Screen capture, durable-memory save, alternate-operation feedback, and per-request limits pass through the same caller allowlist and neutral tool-loop budgets. The matrix executes the production gates, strict registry input validation, screen and memory permission failures, zero-call budget, result clipping, and total-output exhaustion; the approval workflow separately proves denied alternate-operation feedback remains in-band.

Request cancellation, bubble Stop, and dismiss-only notices share the supervisor cancellation token, bounded worker IPC, stale reply-state guard, and cleanup path. The executable request and IPC tests prove delivery and bounded completion; the bubble tests prove stale and non-cancellable UI states remain safe.

GitHub OAuth overrides feed the same device authorization flow as the normal login. The matrix contains browser, expiry, cancellation, provider, network, and rejected-scope failures, while the keyring test proves a returned token cannot silently fall back to plaintext storage.

Settings Test Chat uses the shared route probe and configuration check. The probe tests reject an incomplete route before provider I/O and convert authentication, endpoint, network, model, rate-limit, and unsupported-capability exceptions into a failed diagnostic.

On-demand memory search is gated by the memory setting, caller allowlist, provider tool capability, completion gate, guarded memory-store adapter, and shared request budgets. The matrix executes each gate and the unified harness proves a model that skips the required search is nudged into issuing it.

Browser, ambient-document, and tool-document limits use the supervisor context-policy snapshot and the shared per-result/total tool budget. The runtime matrix disables, denies, empties, and stales sources while enforcing the captured-context cap; the tool matrix proves clipping and total-output exhaustion.

Providers without word timestamps use SpeechBubble's WPM fallback timer. The Settings test verifies provider capability classification, the bubble matrix proves invalid WPM falls back safely and completion stops/reset timers, and the UI watchdog covers a blocked event loop.

Packaged, source, duplicate-instance, runtime-status, autostart, and shutdown entry paths depend on the same repository/install metadata and Python runtime before supervisor lifecycle behavior can succeed. The reusable preflight matrix executes missing/corrupt installation and runtime states; supervisor tests separately cover lock, startup, and cleanup behavior.

First-start setup begins with the same install metadata validation as the reusable development/launch preflight, then runs its component checks. The matrix proves both absent and structurally corrupt installations are rejected before setup reports readiness.

Setup navigation, TTS/STT choices, and idle/listening/thinking/speaking state all run on the same Qt/runtime, supervisor worker, permission, and tray surface. The preflight, startup transaction, and tray dispatch matrices prove each shared dependency failure remains controlled.

Removing an action, invoking the custom prompt, and switching dictation modes all depend on the same native global-hook registration, conflict detector, accessibility fallback, and event-swallow behavior as the primary hotkeys.

macOS helper and Accessibility operations enter the same native selection/focus boundary. Its matrix contains absent features, permission failures, focus changes, and unsupported target applications without returning stale content.

Linux accessibility and display fallbacks are best-effort: the AT-SPI matrix injects absent services plus sandbox/session permission faults and an empty incompatible toolkit tree, while the Qt surface matrix contains compositor refusal.

Shortcut renames now pass the production caller validator before the shared atomic Settings transaction. The UI matrix rejects empty, control-character, and duplicate names; the persistence matrix injects read-only, locked, corrupt, and interrupted stores.

Adding an action row uses the production caller/action validator before Settings persistence. The matrix rejects key conflicts, missing fields, malformed prompt data, and the shared transaction contains write failures.

Editing an action uses the same key/field/prompt validator and Settings transaction. The matrix covers replacement conflicts, invalid edits, stale row metadata, and read-only/corrupt stores without persisting a partial configuration.

Project creation validates its name, deduplicates case-insensitively, and writes through the atomic project store. The matrix rejects empty/invalid values, returns the existing duplicate, injects read-only/locked/interrupted replacement failures, and the corrupt-store test proves fail-closed loading.

Manual fact creation now returns the real insert result and clears typed input only after success. The UI matrix stops empty input, retains invalid/duplicate input, and injects read-only, locked, corrupt, and interrupted stores; the memory-store matrix verifies deduplication and corrupt-store containment.

An exact manually entered model is not trusted until the shared live route probe succeeds. The matrix injects misspelling, account, endpoint, provider-pairing, and route-capability failures and verifies each returns a failed diagnostic.

The Ollama provider uses one readiness/startup manager followed by the shared live route probe. Its matrix executes a server that never becomes ready, a launch failure, wrong remote host/port, missing model, and RAM/VRAM probe failures.

Custom OpenAI-compatible routes now validate an absolute HTTP(S) base URL before client construction, then use the shared live route probe. The matrix injects offline, credential, model, and protocol-compatibility failures as controlled diagnostics.

Both auto-close and zero-timeout modes use the same production timer parser and state-transition cleanup. The overlay matrix executes malformed and zero duration values, retires stale timers, and proves modal-owned cancellation is idempotent; the supervisor watchdog separately detects a blocked UI event loop.

Cancel is a no-write transaction: unsaved controls never invoke the runtime apply callback and do not require reconstructing a possibly stale snapshot. The matrix injects missed dirty state and a failing snapshot, verifies persisted bytes are unchanged, and prevents Settings disposal while an installer or setup wizard is active.

Recovery formatting now has an explicit unresponsive-worker rule plus a diagnostic fallback for sparse or unknown failures. The matrix preserves supplied technical context and always points users to Runtime Status, recent logs, and crash-report capture when no specific rule matches.
